name: getRoll
description:
Roll one or more dice using standard D&D notation and return the individual results and total. Use this for all dice rolls in the game: initiative, attack rolls, damage, saving throws, ability checks, skill checks — anything that requires a die roll. Never simulate or guess dice results; always call this tool.

Supported die sizes: d2, d3, d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20, d100.
Notation examples: '1d20' (single d20), '2d6+3' (two d6 plus 3), '1d20-1' (d20 with negative modifier), '4d6' (four d6, e.g. stat generation).

Advantage/disadvantage: only applies to single d20 rolls. When active, two d20s are rolled and the higher (advantage) or lower (disadvantage) is used. The modifier is then added to the chosen roll.

notation.description: Dice notation in NdS+M format, e.g. '1d20', '2d6+3', '1d8-1'. N=number of dice, S=sides, M=modifier (optional).

advantage.description: If true, roll two d20s and take the higher result. Only applies to single d20 rolls.

disadvantage.description: If true, roll two d20s and take the lower result. Only applies to single d20 rolls.

label.description: Optional label for the roll, e.g. 'Goblin attack roll', 'Fireball damage', 'Stealth check'. Included in the result for clarity.
